Affirmative action is a complex policy that addresses the role of institutional racism and disparities. It acknowledges that structural barriers have historically disadvantaged certain groups, so it aim to correct that. But overly aggressive affirmative action risk promotion of tokenism and could be seen as a way to address the attitude of white supremacy. It ultimately meets the needs of the audience, neither universally miseducated nor nakedly unacceptable.
